title: Connected systems, devices, and methods for monitoring clinical treatments
abstract: Systems, devices, and methods are described for providing, among other things, improvements in treatment of abnormal tissues including cancer, utilizing light activated drug therapy. Improvements include methodologies and technologies for monitoring cancer immunotherapy clinical treatments, analyzing clinical outcomes, improving patient experience, and the like. In an embodiment, a system, includes circuitry configured to exchange clinical information about a user, and circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  The embodiments of the invention in which an exclusive property or privilege is claimed are defined as follows: 1. A system, comprising: circuitry configured to exchange clinical information about a user, and circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information is operably coupled to a sensor array including computing device circuitry and memory circuitry, and is configured to generate quality-of-life information about the user responsive to one or more inputs from the sensor array. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information includes computing device circuitry, memory circuitry, and at least one of transceiver circuitry, transmitter circuitry, and receiver circuitry. 4. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to exchange physiological information with a remote client device; and circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user and the physiological information. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the circuitry configured to exchange physiological information with a remote client device is operably coupled to one or more physiological sensors. 6. The system of claim 4, wherein the circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user includes health state circuitry configured to generate user health state information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user and the physiological information. 7. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to exchange biometric information with a remote client device, and circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user and the biometric information. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the circuitry configured to exchange biometric information with a remote client device is operably coupled to one or more biometric sensor. 9. The system of claim 7, wherein the circuitry configured to exchange biometric information with a remote client device is operably coupled to one or more nodes. 10. The system of claim 7, wherein the circuitry configured to exchange biometric information with a remote client device includes computing device circuitry, memory circuitry, and at least one of transceiver circuitry, transmitter circuitry, and receiver circuitry. 11. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to exchange treatment session information with a remote client device, and circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user and the treatment session information. 12. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to exchange photoactivation treatment information, the photoactivation treatment information including at least one of the total photoactivation treatment time, light intensity, fluence rate, and total joules of energy dose, treatment session information with a remote client device, and circuitry configured to generate quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user and the photoactivation treatment information. 13. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to anonymize quality-of-life information prior to transmitting. 14. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to encrypt quality-of-life information prior to transmitting. 15. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to generate reimbursement information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user. 16. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to generate indirect/direct cost information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user. 17. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to generate decision tree information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user. 18. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to generate treatment regimen information based at least in part on the clinical information about a user. 19. The system of claim 1, further comprising: circuitry configured to transmit, anonymized, and encrypted clinical information. 20. A method, comprising: receiving clinical information about a user, the clinical information including at least one of disease free survival information, duration of response information, event free survival information, number needed to treat (NNT) information, overall response rate information, overall survival information, median overall survival information, progression free survival information, time to next treatment information, time to progression information, time to treatment failure information; receiving physiological information from a physiological sensor of wearable device of the user; and 

 determining quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information and the physiological information about the user. 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising: receiving biometric information from a biometric sensor of wearable device of the user; and determining quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information and the biometric information about the user. 22. The method of claim 20, further comprising: presenting the quality-of-life information via at least one of a visual display device of the wearable device, a haptic device of the wearable device, and an audio output device of the wearable device. 23. The method of claim 20, further comprising: presenting the quality-of-life information, via one or more output devices, directing the user to alter therapy. 24. The method of claim 20, wherein receiving the physiological information from the physiological sensor of the wearable device of the user includes receiving at least one of premorbid condition marker information, biomarker information, change in a biomarker information, and inflammation status information. 25. The method of claim 20, further comprising: anonymizing and encrypting at least one of the clinical information about the user, the physiological information, and quality-of-life information prior to transmitting the at least one of the clinical information about the user, the physiological information, and quality-of-life information. 26. The method of claim 20, further comprising: exchanging anonymized and encrypted information associated with at least one of the clinical information about the user, the physiological information, and quality-of-life information with a remote network. 27. The method of claim 20, further comprising: exchanging at least one of the clinical information about the user, the physiological information, and quality-of-life information with a client device. 28. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (H PAA)-compliant clinical information about the user. 29. The method of claim 20, further comprising: exchanging photoactivation treatment information, the photoactivation treatment information including at least one of the total photoactivation treatment time, light intensity, fluence rate, and total joules of energy dose. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein determining quality-of-life information includes determining quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information, the physiological information about the user, and the photoactivation treatment information. 31. The method of claim 20, further comprising: exchanging user-specific health information, the user-specific health information including at least one of the diet, exercise status, sleep habit information, quality of sleep information, nutritional status information, supplement usage information, general lifestyle information, and premorbid condition information; and determining quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information, the physiological information about the user, and the user-specific health information. 32. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating reimbursement information based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information. 33. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating regulatory requirement information based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information. 34. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating therapy comparison information based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information. 35. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating an alternative therapy protocol based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information. 36. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating survivorship information based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information 37. The method of claim 20, further comprising: determining a therapeutic change based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information. 38. The method of claim 20, further comprising: evaluating a therapeutic protocol, regimen, or treatment based on at least one of the physiological information and the quality-of-life information. 39. The method of claim 20, further comprising: capturing clinical trial information. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein capturing clinical trial information includes receiving one or more audio inputs associated cancer diagnostic information. 41. The method of claim 39, wherein capturing clinical trial information includes receiving one or more audio inputs associated with at least one of a laboratory result, execution of therapy information, procedure compliance information, delivery of care information, and treatment administration information 42. The method of claim 39, wherein capturing clinical trial information includes receiving data representing one or more of medical images, laboratory data, procedure compliance information, and therapeutic implementation information. 43. The method of claim 39, wherein capturing clinical trial information includes receiving data obtained by a microphone array coupled to the wearable device. 44. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating reimbursement information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user. 45. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating indirect/direct cost information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user. 46. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating decision tree information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user. 47. The method of claim 20, further comprising: generating treatment regimen information based at least in part on the clinical information about the user. 48. A clinical trial device, comprising: means for receiving clinical information and physiological information about a user; and means for determining quality-of-life information based at least in part on the clinical information and the physiological information about the user. 49. The clinical trial device of claim 48, further comprising: means for anonymizing, encrypting, and transmitting quality-of-life information. 50. The clinical trial device of claim 48, further comprising: means for exchanging anonymized and encrypted information associated with at least one of the clinical information about the user, the physiological information, and quality-of-life information with a remote network. 51. The clinical trial device of claim 48, wherein the clinical trial device comprises one of a client device, a computer device, a connected device, a desktop 

 computer device, a laptop computer device, a notebook computer device, a managed node device, a mobile device, a remote controller, a smartphone device, and a tablet device. 52. The clinical trial device of claim 48, wherein the clinical trial device comprises one of a wearable device, a wearable health monitor device, and an application interface with a smart device.
